name: Federal Litigation Defenses & Adversary index (civil FRCP + criminal + regulatory)
note: Original index of the defenses and dispositive attacks a federal adversary brings, authored from public federal law (FRCP, FRE, the U.S. Code, and Supreme Court / circuit case law). Powers the central adversarial gate's DOJ/AUSA Prosecutor and Federal Defense Counsel personas — the federal analog of the FL Defense Counsel. Each entry: what to show, who bears the BURDEN, the authority, and what it defeats. 'cat': fed-civil | fed-criminal | fed-regulatory. VERIFY each against current law (circuits split; standards shift).

show: the complaint pleads only labels/conclusions or a 'formulaic recitation'
the well-pleaded facts do not make the claim plausible on its face (more than possible)
defenses.burden: movant (facts taken as true; conclusions not)
defenses.defeats: any federally-pleaded claim lacking factual plausibility
defenses.basis: Bell Atlantic v. Twombly 550 U.S. 544; Ashcroft v. Iqbal 556 U.S. 662; FRCP 12(b)(6)

show: no federal question (§ 1331) and no complete diversity + > $75,000 (§ 1332)
or a non-justiciable / political question
defenses.burden: plaintiff invoking jurisdiction; non-waivable, raised anytime
defenses.defeats: any federal action without a jurisdictional basis

show: no concrete and particularized injury-in-fact (TransUnion: 'no concrete harm, no standing')
no causation/traceability
not redressable
defenses.defeats: statutory-violation suits without concrete harm; speculative-injury claims
defenses.basis: Lujan v. Defenders 504 U.S. 555; Spokeo 578 U.S. 330; TransUnion v. Ramirez 594 U.S. 413

show: no minimum contacts / purposeful availment (specific) and not 'at home' (general)
exercise offends fair play and substantial justice
defenses.defeats: claims vs. a defendant outside the forum's reach
defenses.basis: Int'l Shoe 326 U.S. 310; Daimler v. Bauman 571 U.S. 117; Ford Motor 592 U.S. 351

show: venue is not proper under § 1391 (residence / events / property)
or transfer is warranted (§ 1404) or forum non conveniens

defenses.d: Failure to State a Claim — Rule 9(b) Particularity (fraud)
show: a fraud/mistake claim does not plead the who/what/when/where/how with particularity
(PSLRA) securities fraud does not plead a strong inference of scienter
defenses.defeats: federal fraud, RICO-predicate fraud, securities-fraud claims
defenses.basis: FRCP 9(b); PSLRA 15 U.S.C. § 78u-4(b); Tellabs 551 U.S. 308

show: the expert is unqualified, or the opinion is unreliable (testability, error rate, peer review, general acceptance) or does not fit
(2023 amendment) the proponent did not show reliability by a preponderance
defenses.defeats: claims that depend on inadmissible expert proof
defenses.basis: Daubert 509 U.S. 579; Kumho Tire 526 U.S. 137; FRE 702 (am. 2023)

show: no genuine dispute of material fact
the nonmovant lacks evidence on an essential element (movant may point to the absence)
defenses.basis: FRCP 56; Celotex 477 U.S. 317; Anderson 477 U.S. 242; Matsushita 475 U.S. 574

show: the filing lacks evidentiary or legal support and was not withdrawn within the 21-day safe harbor
or counsel multiplied the proceedings unreasonably/vexatiously
no violation of a constitutional right, OR the right was not 'clearly established' at the time (a case on point)
defenses.defeats: civil-rights damages claims vs. individual officials
defenses.basis: Harlow v. Fitzgerald 457 U.S. 800; Pearson v. Callahan 555 U.S. 223

show: a parallel state proceeding / state-court judgment / ongoing state enforcement bars federal adjudication
defenses.defeats: federal suits paralleling or reviewing state proceedings
defenses.basis: Younger 401 U.S. 37; Colorado River 424 U.S. 800; Rooker-Feldman doctrine

show: the federal claim's limitations period ran (borrowed-state or federal: e.g., § 1983 borrows state PI; securities §§ 1658(b)/13 of '33 Act; RICO 4 yr)
no equitable tolling/discovery
defenses.basis: 28 U.S.C. § 1658; claim-specific periods

show: administrative remedies not exhausted (PLRA / APA / Title VII-EEOC), or the dispute is unripe/moot
defenses.basis: case + statute specific

show: the indictment was returned more than 5 years after the offense (default) — longer for some (tax 6 yr; wire/bank fraud affecting a FI 10 yr)
no tolling (fugitive § 3290)
the offense was not committed in the charging district (Const. Art. III §2; 6th Am.)
defenses.burden: government must prove venue by a preponderance
defenses.basis: U.S. Const.; FRCrP 18; Smith v. United States 599 U.S. 236

defenses.d: Insufficiency of the Evidence (mens rea / element)
show: the government cannot prove each element beyond a reasonable doubt — esp. the required INTENT (willfulness, specific intent to defraud)
mere negligence/good faith negates intent
defenses.defeats: wire/bank/securities fraud, FCPA, tax-evasion, AML counts
defenses.basis: FRCrP 29; Cheek (willfulness); Ruan (mens rea)

show: the defendant honestly believed the conduct was lawful / no intent to defraud
reliance on counsel/accountant (full disclosure + reliance)
defenses.basis: case law; Cheek v. United States 498 U.S. 192

show: an unlawful search/seizure (no warrant/exception, overbroad, stale, Franks misstatement)
the evidence is fruit of the poisonous tree
defenses.defeats: the tainted evidence (often guts the case)
defenses.basis: U.S. Const. amend. IV; FRCrP 12(b)(3)(C); Mapp; Wong Sun

show: the government suppressed material exculpatory/impeachment evidence
a reasonable probability of a different result
defenses.defeats: the conviction / forces disclosure or dismissal
defenses.basis: Brady v. Maryland 373 U.S. 83; Giglio 405 U.S. 150

show: a second prosecution/punishment for the same offense (Blockburger same-elements), or one count charging multiple offenses (duplicity) / one offense in multiple counts (multiplicity)
defenses.basis: U.S. Const. amend. V; Blockburger 284 U.S. 299

show: government inducement of the crime
the defendant lacked predisposition to commit it
defenses.burden: defendant produces inducement; government proves predisposition beyond reasonable doubt
defenses.basis: Jacobson v. United States 503 U.S. 540

show: the indictment fails to allege an essential element, is constructively amended, or there was prosecutorial misconduct before the grand jury
defenses.basis: U.S. Const. amend. V; FRCrP 7, 12; Stirone

defenses.d: SEC — Scienter / Materiality / Reliance / Loss Causation
show: no scienter (intent or severe recklessness) for 10b-5; the misstatement is immaterial; no reliance (rebut Basic presumption); no loss causation
defenses.basis: Ernst & Ernst v. Hochfelder; Basic v. Levinson; Dura Pharm.

show: the SEC's penalty/disgorgement claim accrued > 5 years before suit (Gabelli); disgorgement exceeds net profits / is not for victims (Liu)
(Jarkesy) SEC fraud penalties require an Article III jury, not the in-house ALJ
defenses.defeats: stale SEC penalty/disgorgement claims; in-house adjudication of fraud penalties
defenses.basis: 28 U.S.C. § 2462; Gabelli 568 U.S. 442; Liu 591 U.S. 71; SEC v. Jarkesy 603 U.S. (2024)

show: no jurisdictional nexus (issuer/domestic concern/territorial)
the payment is a bona fide facilitating ('grease') payment or a lawful promotional expense
no corrupt intent/knowledge of the foreign official's status
defenses.basis: 15 U.S.C. §§ 78dd-1 to -3; FCPA Resource Guide

show: no 'willful' violation (BSA reporting) — voluntary, intentional violation of a known legal duty
no knowledge the funds were proceeds of specified unlawful activity (§ 1956)
structuring lacked intent to evade (§ 5324)
no willfulness — a good-faith misunderstanding of the law (even unreasonable) negates willfulness for tax crimes
good-faith reliance on a competent professional
defenses.basis: Cheek v. United States 498 U.S. 192; 26 U.S.C. §§ 7201, 7206

show: no distinct 'enterprise' separate from the persons; no 'pattern' (continuity + relationship) of racketeering; insufficient predicate acts; no proximate cause of the injury
defenses.basis: 18 U.S.C. §§ 1961-1968; H.J. Inc.; Reves; Anza
